title: EU Clinches Deal to Roll Back AI Restrictions
summary: EU legislators reached a landmark political agreement to delay high-risk AI rules by more than a year and carve out industrial AI from the AI Act's scope.
content: Restrictions on high-risk uses of artificial intelligence in the EU will be postponed until December 2027 under a deal agreed by EU legislators on May 7, marking the first significant rollback of rules in the digital space. The deal, struck after overnight negotiations, also largely exempts industrial AI — used by companies like Siemens and Bosch — from the AI Act, requiring them to comply only with separate EU machinery rules instead. Companies will also receive a three-month grace period on new requirements to watermark AI-generated content, though this was shorter than the six months originally proposed. The agreement additionally bans AI systems designed to generate sexualized deepfakes of identifiable individuals and AI-generated child sexual abuse material. Commission President Ursula von der Leyen welcomed the deal as providing a "simple, innovation-friendly environment" for AI in Europe while strengthening citizen protections.

title: OpenAI Launches GPT-Realtime-2 and New Voice Intelligence Models
summary: OpenAI introduces three new real-time audio models including GPT-Realtime-2, its first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning, plus live translation and streaming transcription.
content: OpenAI released three new audio models on May 7, 2026, designed to move voice AI from simple call-and-response toward interfaces that can listen, reason, translate, and act in real time. GPT-Realtime-2 is OpenAI's first voice model with GPT-5-class reasoning, featuring a 128K context window, parallel tool calls, adjustable reasoning effort levels, and a 15.2% improvement on audio intelligence benchmarks over its predecessor. GPT-Realtime-Translate supports live speech translation across 70+ input languages into 13 output languages, enabling real-time multilingual conversations for customer support, education, and global media. GPT-Realtime-Whisper is a streaming transcription model built for low-latency speech-to-text, transcribing audio as people speak for meeting notes, live captions, and voice agents. Early partners including Zillow, Deutsche Telekom, and Priceline are already integrating the models into production voice experiences.

title: IBM Study: Chief AI Officer Roles Surge from 26% to 76% in One Year
summary: A global IBM survey of 2,000 CEOs finds organizations are dramatically reshaping their C-suites around AI, with CAIO adoption tripling in a single year.
content: A new IBM Institute for Business Value study of 2,000 CEOs across 33 geographies finds that 76% of organizations now have a Chief AI Officer in 2026, up from just 26% a year ago, as AI reshapes enterprise leadership structures. Organizations with an AI-first approach to C-suite design have scaled 10% more AI initiatives enterprise-wide than peers, while 64% of surveyed CEOs say they are now comfortable making major strategic decisions based on AI-generated input. By 2030, CEOs expect 48% of operational decisions where consistency can be codified will be made by AI without human intervention, compared to 25% today. Despite the optimism, only 25% of the workforce is currently using AI regularly on the job, even though 86% of CEOs believe their employees have the skills to collaborate with AI. Between 2026 and 2028, organizations expect 29% of employees to require reskilling for a different role and 53% to need upskilling to perform their current roles more effectively.

title: Enterprise AI Is Stalling: 46% of Initiatives Fall Short Despite Rising Investment
summary: A new survey of 800 U.S. business leaders finds that nearly half of enterprise AI programs fail to meet expectations, revealing a widening gap between investment and real-world results.
content: Coastal's 2026 AI Operations Report, conducted with Oxford Economics across 800 U.S. business and technology leaders, finds that while 74% of organizations are increasing AI investment, 46% say their initiatives have not met expectations and only a small minority report measurable business value. Data quality and access remain persistent obstacles — 70% report issues during AI setup and 73% face the same problems running AI in production. Employee readiness is not the bottleneck: 77% of organizations say employees are eager to use AI, yet 73% still struggle with adoption due to lack of trust, poor workflow fit, or unclear outputs. Structural gaps compound the problem: just 26% of organizations begin AI initiatives with a clearly defined business problem, and only one in six has a dedicated AI or transformation team. The report concludes that AI success now depends less on launching new capabilities and more on treating AI as a continuous operating function with clear ownership, defined roadmaps, and ongoing management.

title: AWS Launches Agent Toolkit to Help AI Coding Agents Build on AWS
summary: Amazon Web Services releases the Agent Toolkit for AWS, a production-ready suite of 40+ skills, a fully-managed MCP server, and agent plugins to help AI coding agents work reliably on AWS infrastructure.
content: AWS launched the Agent Toolkit for AWS on May 6, 2026, a free production-ready toolkit that addresses a common failure mode in AI coding workflows: agents that struggle with complex multi-service AWS tasks, rely on outdated documentation, and are difficult to govern in production. The toolkit includes over 40 validated agent skills covering infrastructure-as-code, storage, analytics, serverless, containers, and AI services, each rigorously evaluated to help agents follow AWS best practices rather than improvising. A fully-managed MCP server is also now generally available, offering IAM-based guardrails, Amazon CloudWatch and AWS CloudTrail observability, and sandboxed code execution for multi-step operations. Three agent plugins bundle the MCP server and curated skills into single installs for common use cases: AWS Core for full-stack app development, AWS Data Analytics for data pipelines, and AWS Agents for building production-ready agents on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The toolkit is available at no additional charge, with users paying only for the AWS resources their agents consume.

title: Adobe Launches AI Productivity Agent for Acrobat, Turning PDFs into Interactive Experiences
summary: Adobe unveiled a new agentic productivity interface for Acrobat that transforms static documents into AI-powered, interactive sharing experiences using a new PDF Spaces format.
content: Adobe launched its new productivity agent on May 6, 2026, bringing decades of Acrobat document intelligence into a single agentic interface that can generate presentations, podcasts, social posts, and interactive experiences directly from PDFs and documents. The agent powers a new PDF Spaces format in Acrobat, where senders can combine files, links, and notes, then customize an AI Assistant that represents their tone and intent so recipients can ask questions and explore content interactively — without needing an account to view. The productivity agent works behind the scenes to generate titles, summaries, audio overviews, and engagement insights, automatically updating shared experiences whenever source documents change. Major media and creator partners including VICE News, Kid Cudi, journalist Jessica Yellin, and event planner Mindy Weiss are already using PDF Spaces for audience engagement and storytelling. The new capabilities are available now in Acrobat Express and Acrobat Studio, part of Adobe's broader agentic strategy alongside its creative agent, with the two designed to work in tandem across documents, data, and systems.
